reasoning: As of late July 2025, the Wikipedia article "List of infectious diseases" correctly lists "No" for the vaccine status of Human metapneumovirus (hMPV). A review of current clinical trials reveals that the most advanced vaccine candidates for hMPV, such as Moderna's mRNA-1653 and mRNA-1365, are only in Phase 1 or Phase 2 clinical trials. Given that standard vaccine development and regulatory approval timelines require large-scale Phase 3 trials mapping over several years—and even fast-tracked approvals take roughly a year—it is practically impossible for an hMPV vaccine to be fully developed, approved, and correctly recorded on Wikipedia before January 2026. The only potential pathway for a "YES" resolution on these dates would be un-reverted vandalism or an erroneous edit on the Wikipedia page exactly on the check dates. Thus, my baseline probability is at the lowest permitted threshold, with slight artificial increases applied to the more distant dates strictly to satisfy the requirement that uncertainty must increase over time.
